resumes.title: Platform Product Manager
resumes.why: Pasted JD, no job-board link yet. 91% on the platform/API/data SKILL keywords (20/22: platform, reusable, shared services, API integration, backend systems, systems thinking, fraud/verification, consent, payments, data-driven, prioritization, adoption, business outcomes all covered from real work -- 6-gateway payment integration, in-house VC platform, verification/fraud flow, omnichannel support, backend automation, BI). Keyword gaps: configurable + platform architecture (platform-PM depth). IMPORTANT -- this is an honest STRETCH, and the 91% reflects transferable skills, not overall fit: (1) domain is fintech LENDING (Personal/Gold/Business Loans, Credit Cards, EMI) -- his platform/payments experience is B2C EdTech, a real domain transfer; (2) it's a dedicated Platform-PM role (building shared platform services as the whole job) vs his product/journey ownership; (3) specific named capabilities in the JD -- rule engine, UPI Mandate, selfie liveness/face verification -- he hasn't built (they're listed as examples, not requirements). Defensible application on platform/API/data strength, but not a slam-dunk. Auto-fit to one full page (93.1%).

resumes.title: Product Manager
resumes.why: Pasted JD, no job-board link yet. LOCATION FIT -- based in Gurugram/Noida (his #1 priority). 89% on Essential-skill keywords (24/27) -- strong, well-transferable: product strategy + development lifecycle, delivery discipline (Agile/JIRA/backlog), analytical & data-driven (SQL, Power BI, funnel/MI), customer-journey/pain-points, stakeholder management, cross-functional, market/competitor analysis, commercial & financial acumen, risk & control, rapid prototyping, AI-enabled tools all covered from real work. 3 keyword gaps, of which 2 are just tool NAMES he has equivalents for: Tableau (has Power BI), HUE (has SQL) -- practical fit higher than 89. Only true gap is Consumer Duty / ORAC (UK-banking governance frameworks). DESIRABLE-only gaps (not scored): loans/banking domain, Agentic AI, Spec-Driven Development. This is a much more realistic fit than a specialized fintech role -- it's a general digital-PM role with a governance overlay, in his target city. Auto-fit to one full page (95.2%).

resumes.title: Manager, Enterprise Payments Product Development
resumes.why: Pasted JD, no job-board link yet. 88% on skill keywords (21/24: product development/management, delivery, release plans, Agile/JIRA, user stories, features, capabilities, payments, stakeholder mgmt, cross-functional, analytical, problem solving, scale, performance metrics, modernization, drive change, team leadership, trade-offs, roadmap all covered from real work). BIGGEST STRETCH OF THE BATCH -- the 88% measures transferable delivery/payments SKILLS and materially OVERSTATES the real fit: this is an ENTERPRISE PAYMENTS-INFRASTRUCTURE role on a $1.6T network (clearing & settlement, bank-rail integrations, legacy-to-cloud modernization, scaled Agile/SAFe, leading a product org across 170 markets). His payments experience is B2C payment-GATEWAY integration (Razorpay/PayU/etc.), NOT enterprise money-movement infra -- a different world. Keyword gaps reflect this: clearing & settlement, cloud (legacy->cloud migration), scaled Agile. Honest recommendation: only apply if deliberately pivoting toward enterprise payments; the delivery + multi-gateway-payments + modernization-of-flows story is the angle, but expect the domain gap to be the deciding factor, not the resume. Auto-fit to one full page (94.8%).
